title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense · Independent Container Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer daemon operations, upgrades, recovery, cleanup, orchestration boundary, and capstone defense into a new production Docker operations and release defense without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of production Docker operations and release defense with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Daemon Config Live Restore
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Daemon Config Live Restore.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, dns, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Daemon Config Live Restore, keep Docker Daemon Config Live Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.instruction:

The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-capstone-production-defense-jp2y25
  - competency: docker-capstone-production-defense
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: capstone production defense
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: remove dependency readiness while preserving start order
    retained-proof: effective user capability filesystem and network inspection
    case-key: jp2y25
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Docker Capstone Production Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Docker Capstone Production Defense output and hide the violated production Docker operations and release defense assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Docker Capstone Production Defense.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Capstone Production Defense for production Docker operations and release defense.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Docker Capstone Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Docker Capstone Production Defense through the concrete production Docker operations and release defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense, keep Docker Capstone Production Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Capstone Production Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A lab board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Capstone Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Passing isolated technical checks removes the need for an end-to-end production defense.
